data.title: Labor cost growth cools markedly in Q2, productivity rises more than expected
data.description: Nonfarm labor productivity increased 2.4% Q/Q in Q2, faster than the +1.9% expected and accelerating -1.8% in Q1 (revised from -1.5%), according to data released by the U.S. Department of Labor on Thursday.

data.title: CME declares $1.25 dividend
data.description:
• CME (NASDAQ:CME) declared $1.25/share quarterly dividend, in line with previous.
  • Forward yield 1.78%
  • Payable Sept. 25; for shareholders of record Sept. 9; ex-div Sept. 9.
  • See CME Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Jazz Pharma wins FDA nod for Modeyso against rare brain tumor
data.description:
• Jazz Pharmaceuticals (NASDAQ:JAZZ) has received the U.S. FDA's approval for Modeyso (dordaviprone), an orally administered once-weekly therapy for a rare brain tumor known as diffuse midline glioma, as a late-line option.
  • Specifically, the agency has granted accelerated approval for Modeyso therapy following previous treatment for those aged at least a year with progressive diffuse midline glioma harboring the H3 K27M mutation.
  • Continuous approval for the drug against the indication will be subject to data from the company's Phase 3 ACTION confirmatory trial, which is currently underway, with a primary completion date in August 2026.
  • The approval was backed by data from five open-label clinical studies, in which 50 patients with recurrent H3K27M-mutant diffuse midline glioma indicated an overall response rate of 22% after receiving Modeyso.

data.title: Addus HomeCare names Heather Dixon as Chief Operating Officer
data.description:
• Addus HomeCare (NASDAQ:ADUS) Thursday named Heather Dixon as President and Chief Operating Officer.
  • Dixon is expected to join the company in that role in mid-September.
  • W. Bradley Bickham, the company’s current President and Chief Operating Officer, has been appointed advisor to the Chief Executive Officer upon Dixon joining and has agreed to serve in that role until his previously announced retirement in March 2026.

data.title: Intel dips after Trump calls for resignation of chipmaker's CEO
data.description:
Shares of Intel (NASDAQ:INTC) fell about 3% premarket on Thursday after President Donald Trump called for the resignation of the chipmaker's new CEO Lip-Bu Tan.

"The CEO of INTEL is highly CONFLICTED and must resign, immediately. There is no other solution to this problem. Thank you for your attention to this problem!" said Trump in a post on Truth Social.

Trump's remarks comes on the heels of a letter sent by U.S. Republican Senator Tom Cotton to Intel's board chair asking questions about Tan's relations with Chinese companies and a recent criminal case involving his former company Cadence Design Systems (CDNS).

Intel did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.

Since his appointment, Tan has been carrying out changes to revamp the U.S. chipmaking company. Last month, Intel said it intends to cut 15% jobs and plans to end the year with a global headcount of around 75,000 as a part of sweeping restructuring under Tan.

On Wednesday, Trump said he plans to impose a 100% tariff on semiconductor imports, with an exemption for companies that manufacture or commit to manufacturing in the U.S.

data.title: Hertz Global Holdings Non-GAAP EPS of -$0.34 beats by $0.07, revenue of $2.19B beats by $20M

data.title: Earnings Snapshot: Cheniere Energy surpasses Q2 estimates; updates FY outlook
PennyMac Financial proposes $650M senior notes offering
data.description:
• PennyMac Financial Services (NYSE:PFSI) and its subsidiaries plan to offer $650 million aggregate principal amount of Senior Notes due 2034.
  • Proceeds from the offering will be used to repay borrowings under the company’s secured MSR facilities, other secured indebtedness, and for other general corporate purposes.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Onex GAAP EPS of $3.30
data.description:
• Onex press release (OTCPK:ONEXF): Q2 GAAP EPS of $3.30.
  • Onex’ private equity investments had net gains of $184 million or a 4% return in the second quarter of 2025 (Q2 2024: $121 million or a 2% return). Investments in Credit strategies generated net gains of $33 million or a 4% return in the quarter (Q2 2024: $20 million or a 3% return).

data.title: BlackRock TCP Capital NII of $0.31 misses by $0.01
data.description:
• BlackRock TCP Capital press release (NASDAQ:TCPC): Q2 NII of $0.31 misses by $0.01.
  • Total investment income of $51.46M (-28.1% Y/Y) misses by $3.04M.
  • Net asset value per share was $8.71 as of June 30, 2025 compared to $9.18 as of March 31, 2025, driven largely by markdowns on previously restructured portfolio companies.
